Name: dynamic_latency_optimization
Title: Dynamic Latency Optimization for Real-Time Machine Learning Inference
Short Hypothesis: Can real-time latency optimization techniques, informed by hardware-level profiling, significantly enhance inference speed without compromising accuracy in machine learning models deployed on commodity GPUs?
Related Work: Existing literature, such as the work on Spectral Latent Steering and T2DRL frameworks, focuses on optimizing inference but often employs abstract theoretical models or complex algorithms that lack empirical validation on hardware. This proposal differentiates itself by emphasizing direct latency measurements and hardware profiling, aiming for practical, deployable solutions that can be immediately implemented in real-world systems.
Abstract: In the pursuit of enhancing real-time inference performance for machine learning models, this proposal explores dynamic latency optimization techniques that leverage hardware-level profiling metrics. By analyzing GPU performance characteristics such as memory bandwidth utilization, cache behavior, and kernel occupancy, we aim to develop a framework that dynamically allocates resources and adjusts model inference pathways in response to real-time performance metrics. This framework will prioritize empirical measurement and reproducibility, using profiling tools to capture wall-clock latencies and throughput under varying conditions. Our approach will include implementing lightweight intervention strategies—such as adaptive kernel fusion and dynamic layer skipping—to streamline the inference process while maintaining accuracy. Through rigorous benchmarking across multiple models and datasets, we anticipate demonstrating significant reductions in latency, measured in p50/p99 metrics, without sacrificing model performance. This research will contribute to a deeper understanding of hardware-software interactions during inference and provide actionable insights for optimizing machine learning deployments in latency-sensitive environments.
Experiments: Benchmark a baseline model using standard inference techniques to establish performance metrics.
Implement a dynamic resource allocation strategy based on real-time profiling data, adjusting memory and compute allocation dynamically during inference.
Introduce lightweight interventions such as adaptive kernel fusion and layer skipping, measuring their impact on latency and throughput.
Evaluate the performance of the optimized inference framework across different models and input sizes, reporting p50/p99 latency improvements and tokens-per-second throughput.
Conduct A/B tests comparing the optimized model against the baseline across multiple GPUs to assess reproducibility and generalization of results.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The performance gains may vary significantly across different hardware configurations, limiting generalizability.
Measurement overhead from profiling could introduce additional latencies that negate some of the gains.
Dynamic adjustments may lead to instability in inference performance if not implemented carefully.
The focus on empirical optimization may overlook broader architectural improvements that could yield larger performance benefits.
